comment_text.sn35.111:1.1: This is similar to SN 35.26, as is the next discourse.

root_text.sn35.111:1.1: “Cakkhuṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.111:1.6: manaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn35.111:1.7: Cakkhuñca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya …pe…
root_text.sn35.111:1.10: manaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.111:1.1: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding the eye, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.111:1.2: Without directly knowing and completely understanding the ear …
translation_text.sn35.111:1.6: mind, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.111:1.7: By directly knowing and completely understanding the eye, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.
translation_text.sn35.111:1.8: By directly knowing and completely understanding the ear … nose … tongue …
translation_text.sn35.111:1.10: mind, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”
